flashcards.definition: A key chemistry concept about naming covalent compounds.
Prefix meaning '2' used in naming covalent compounds (e.g., dioxide = 2 oxygen atoms).
CO₂ — covalent compound named using prefixes: carbon + di (2) + oxide.
N₂O — systematic covalent name: di (2 N) + nitrogen + mono (1 O) + oxide. Common name: laughing gas.
The prefix 'mono-' is dropped from the first element in a covalent compound name but kept on the second.
